summary: Per-test diagnosis of the P05 builder-vs-witness conflict; produce remediation specs for the builder lane

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: Only this: (a) a Postgres client using a builder-supplied secret GRAPH_KERNEL_DATABASE_URL could commit rows that the test itself inserted via raw SQL (compiled_plans, graph_motor_run_bindings, node_attempts, leases, outbox, run_events) and re-read them on a second client connection in the same process — i.e., transaction durability across a client reconnect, which any functioning Postgres satisfies trivially; and (b) an unauthenticated GET /healthz returned ok:true with migration_version=002_graph_kernel_roles_migrations_v1 and migrations [001, 002]. It does NOT evidence a database restart, a Railway service restart, durability of service-produced state (all rows were test-fabricated, bypassing the kernel write path), that the DB queried is the manifest DB supabase:tkgpapowwplupyekpivy (no non-secret identity captured), or that the responding service runs the audited code (healthz at audit time exposed no commit SHA — witness receipt: deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz). Every evidence field is producer-self-reported; the row-level values the PASS gate actually checked (fencing_generation, outbox status, run_events count) are not in the artifact, so a third party cannot re-derive the verdict.
internal_contradictions: Claim vs mechanism: test is named 01_database_restart and P07_REPORT rows it as 'Database and restart: PASS', but the producing script (run-p07-live-acceptance.ts test1_database_restart) performs only client.end() + new client.connect() — no database restart, no service restart; the artifact's own note concedes 'state retained across reconnect'.

Status vs evidence sufficiency: artifact asserts PASS on an ok-predicate requiring binding-row presence, fencing_generation===1, outbox status in an allowed set, and run_events count>=1, yet none of those observed values appear in the evidence block — the artifact asserts checks its own evidence does not contain.

Builder P07_VERDICT.json declares all 9 tests plain PASS ('all 9 live acceptance tests passed against real staging') while the same bundle's live-tests.json embeds dispatch {ok:true, delivered:0, failed:2, dlq:0} in test 02 and last_error 'cf_queue_publish_failed status=400' in test 06 — embedded delivery failures under a blanket-PASS narrative degrade the credibility 01's PASS inherits.
Scope/label mismatch: the P0.7 verdict file in proofs/p07 declares verdict 'PASS_P05', conflating commissioning phases.

Environment binding is self-attested: environment-manifest.json asserts secrets_present.GRAPH_KERNEL_DATABASE_URL=true and db=supabase:tkgpapowwplupyekpivy, but nothing in the 01 artifact binds the actual connection used to that project (no current_database()/project-ref capture).

Temporal unbinding confirmed live: current /healthz now reports migration 003 and commit_sha 7a64a355..., while the artifact pinned 002 with no SHA — the deployed surface drifted post-audit with no way to attribute the artifact's observations to any specific deploy.
diagnosis.witness_rationale: PASS_WEAK is the reconstruction of: 'the underlying durability claim is probably true, but the artifact proves far less than it says and none of it is independently attributable.' Three legs: (1) Overstated claim — the test title and report promise restart survivability; the script delivers a same-process client reconnect over rows the test itself inserted, which is close to a tautology for any ACID store and never exercises the kernel's own write path. (2) Self-reported, non-re-derivable evidence — no HTTP transcript, no timestamps, no row snapshots, no DB identity; the PASS boolean is computed inside the producer and only the conclusion is exported, violating the author!=subject / re-derivable-by-third-party standard. (3) Attribution break — per the witness receipt, /healthz exposed no commit SHA (deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz), so even the genuinely-live part of the evidence (health exposing migration_version) cannot be bound to the audited merge 060c5223; only the SCHEMA was externally bound (migration checksums 001/002 matched origin/main byte-for-byte). That checksum match is exactly what keeps this above FAIL/INDETERMINATE: one leg of the evidence has real external grounding, and the reconnect check is internally coherent with no sign of fabrication. Hence: weak-but-probably-true = PASS_WEAK, while the builder's plain PASS is indefensible because it certifies a restart property the artifact cannot support.
diagnosis.remediation: Builder lane must re-run test 01 as a genuinely witnessable restart proof and re-submit as 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' (never self-PASS): (1) Create the state under test through the service path (signed /internal/v1/graph-kernel/trigger), not raw SQL, so durability of kernel-produced state is what is proven. (2) Perform a real restart of the deployed surface (Railway service redeploy/restart; if the claim is DB restart, restart the DB) and capture /healthz BEFORE and AFTER verbatim: worker_id / build_id / railway_deployment_id must differ (proves restart occurred) while commit_sha must be identical and equal to the audited SHA (proves same code) — the endpoint now exposes commit_sha (live: 7a64a355), so this binding is available. (3) Record re-derivable evidence: full timestamped HTTP transcripts, the exact SQL queries with full result rows (binding row, fencing_generation, outbox status, run_events count) before and after restart, and a non-secret DB identity capture (e.g., select current_database(), plus Supabase project ref) binding the connection to the manifest DB. (4) Leave the test rows in place and list their keys so a read-only witness can re-query them. (5) Pin the proof-producing commit and a sha256 manifest of the bundle inside the artifact; fix the verdict-file label (P0.7, not PASS_P05) and stop emitting plain PASS from the producer — PASS is the witness's word.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Artifact contains /healthz responses captured before and after an actual restart, verbatim with UTC timestamps, where build_id/railway_deployment_id/worker_id differ across the restart and commit_sha is present, identical on both sides, and equal to the audited merge SHA recorded in the artifact.

State under test was created via signed service API calls (request+response transcripts included), not direct SQL inserts, and the artifact includes the full post-restart query results (graph_motor_run_bindings row, leases.fencing_generation, outbox.status, run_events count) so the PASS predicate can be recomputed from the artifact alone.

Artifact binds the DB connection to the declared environment with non-secret identity evidence (current_database(), Supabase project ref tkgpapowwplupyekpivy) matching environment-manifest.json, without exposing the connection string.
Migration state is bound both ways: schema_migrations versions AND checksums in the artifact match /healthz's reported migrations and match origin/main files byte-for-byte at the pinned SHA.
The persisted test rows (runId et al.) are enumerated in the artifact and retained, so a read-only third party can re-query staging and confirm existence without rerunning anything.

The artifact self-identifies: proof-producing commit SHA, bundle sha256 manifest, UTC execution window, and the label 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' — final PASS appears only in an independent witness receipt (author != subject).

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: The artifact evidences only a partial, harness-simulated path on an unidentifiable service build: (1) a signed trigger returned 200 and created run_4ef3f648ad1f442a9dc1b9e8e2ba06e9 with plan_hash cf3ef000...; (2) a dispatch-once call whose own payload shows the outbox->CF-queue leg FAILED completely (delivered=0, failed=2, dlq=0; corroborated by test 06 in the same bundle: last_error="cf_queue_publish_failed status=400" and an identical baseline_dispatch object, proving CF queue publish was returning 400 at commissioning time); (3) a CLAIMED/IDLE_PLAN_SETTLED settle produced by the test script itself POSTing a direct HMAC-signed wake to /internal/v1/graph-kernel/wake -- the script comment reads "Direct wake execute (CF consumer may also fire)" -- i.e. the harness impersonated the CF consumer rather than observing it. The producing script's PASS predicate is `trig.json.ok && settled` and never inspects the dispatch result, so a 100%-failed queue dispatch still yields PASS. No queue_message_id, no CF-consumer transcript, no ACK evidence, no DB-row snapshot for the run, no timestamp, and no producing-commit binding appears in the artifact. Per the witness receipt, deployed_sha was UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz at audit time (migration checksums bind only the schema), and healthz today reports commit_sha=7a64a355 with migration 003 -- a drifted environment -- so even the harness-mediated settle cannot be attributed to the audited merge SHA 060c5223. Net: the artifact proves the trigger endpoint and the direct-wake endpoint respond on some unidentified build, and simultaneously proves the queue-mediated Motor path was broken.
internal_contradictions: status:"PASS" while the embedded note in the same JSON records dispatch delivered=0 failed=2 -- the artifact self-documents total failure of the queue-delivery leg it exists to prove

Test is named/claimed as "Complete Motor path" (P07_REPORT: trigger -> outbox -> queue -> CF consumer -> wake -> settle) but the only wake evidenced is an out-of-band direct HTTP wake injected by the test harness; the CF consumer appears nowhere in the evidence

P07_REPORT claims architecture laws "Queue wakes only", "At-least-once delivery", and "CF ACKs only on CLAIMED|ALREADY_*|DUPLICATE_WAKE" were enforced, yet this artifact shows zero queue deliveries and a wake that did not come from the queue -- delivery was zero-times, not at-least-once, and the wake was not a queue wake

dispatch.ok:true coexists with delivered:0/failed:2 -- "ok" reflects only that the dispatch endpoint responded, and the producer's PASS predicate (trig.json.ok && settled) structurally ignores the delivered/failed counters, so the grader could never fail on a broken queue

Bundle-internal corroboration of the break: test 06 evidence in live-tests.json shows cf_queue_publish_failed status=400 and a baseline_dispatch identical to test 02's failed dispatch, proving the queue publish path was broken during the whole commissioning window; test 04 ("duplicate queue delivery") likewise contains no queue delivery -- it is a second direct HTTP wake, consistent with the witness's INDETERMINATE grade there

P07_VERDICT claims PASS_P05 = "all 9 live acceptance tests passed against real staging", but the real staging queue integration demonstrably did not work at test time; the tests passed against a harness that simulated the missing leg

Attribution gap: the artifact carries no deployed commit SHA, no timestamp, and no producing-commit reference; healthz at audit time exposed no commit (witness: deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz) and healthz now reports commit_sha=7a64a355 with migration 003 vs the artifact's 002 -- the environment has drifted, so nothing binds this PASS to the audited merge SHA 060c5223
diagnosis.witness_rationale: The witness FAIL is not merely "insufficient evidence" -- the artifact contains affirmative evidence of failure of the specific leg the test exists to prove. A "complete Motor path" claim requires the queue-mediated wake (trigger -> outbox -> CF queue -> CF consumer -> signed wake -> lease claim -> settle). The artifact's own counters show 0 of 2 outbox messages were delivered to the queue, and the settle was produced by the test harness impersonating the consumer with a direct wake. Under the witness standard (self-declared PASS proves nothing; evidence must be internally consistent, externally bound, and re-derivable by a third party), this artifact fails all three prongs: it is internally contradictory (PASS + delivered=0/failed=2), externally unbound (no deployed SHA, no queue message id, no consumer transcript, no DB row, no timestamp), and a third party re-deriving the conclusion from the artifact reaches FAIL, not PASS. The deployed-code unbinding independently caps any grade at PASS_WEAK, but here the embedded counters push it below that: the claim as stated ("complete path passed on real staging") is contradicted by the producer's own evidence, so FAIL -- not PASS_WEAK or INDETERMINATE -- is the defensible grade. The builder's plain PASS is an artifact of a grading predicate that never reads the dispatch outcome.
diagnosis.remediation: Builder lane must (1) fix the CF queue publish 400 (the cf_queue_publish_failed error in the dispatch path -- likely queue binding/auth/payload contract between Railway dispatch-once and the CF queue) rather than weakening the test; (2) harden the test 02 grader so PASS requires dispatch.delivered >= 1 AND the settling wake to be attributable to the CF consumer (assert outbox row reached OUTBOX_DELIVERED with a non-null queue_message_id, and the wake request carried a consumer-identifying marker), with any direct harness wake either removed or recorded as a separate SIMULATED leg that cannot upgrade the verdict; (3) bind the run to code: healthz now exposes commit_sha/build_id/migration list -- capture the full healthz body inside the artifact at run time and require commit_sha to equal the audited merge SHA; (4) re-run the live acceptance against the pinned deploy and re-emit the artifact with externally checkable evidence (DB row snapshots for the run's outbox, run_events, node_attempts, and receipt; queue_message_id; UTC timestamps; producing commit). Then resubmit as SUBMITTED for independent verification -- the witness re-grades from the artifact alone. Also fix the P07_VERDICT aggregation claim that all tests ran \"against real staging\" while any leg is harness-simulated.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Artifact embeds the full /healthz response captured at run start AND run end, with commit_sha present and equal to the audited/pinned deploy SHA, plus migration list -- binding service code and schema to the run window

Artifact records the dispatch-once response with delivered >= 1 and failed == 0 for the run's outbox message, plus a DB snapshot of the outbox row showing status=OUTBOX_DELIVERED with a non-null queue_message_id (proving the message actually entered the CF queue)

The settling wake is attributable to the CF consumer, not the harness: artifact includes the CF consumer's delivery evidence (consumer log line or wake request metadata carrying the queue_message_id / consumer identity) matched to the wake that returned CLAIMED, and the harness issues no direct wake for this run (or labels any such call SIMULATED and excludes it from grading)

Artifact includes read-only DB row snapshots keyed by graph_run_id: graph_motor_run_bindings row, ordered run_events sequence (RUN_QUEUED -> ... -> settle event), node_attempts terminal status, and the run receipt row -- sufficient for a third party to re-derive IDLE_PLAN_SETTLED from the ledger without calling any endpoint

Artifact carries UTC timestamps for every step, the producing script commit SHA, and a grading predicate that mechanically requires all of the above (a grader that would emit FAIL if delivered=0), so status can be recomputed from embedded evidence alone
P07_VERDICT aggregation only claims PASS_P05 when every per-test artifact meets its own binding criteria; any harness-simulated leg is labeled and demotes the aggregate

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: The artifact (proofs/p07/04_duplicate_queue_delivery.json) evidences only this: the test runner made two consecutive direct HTTP POSTs to /internal/v1/graph-kernel/wake with an identical body, and self-reports that the first returned "CLAIMED" and the second "ALREADY_SETTLED". Per the producing script (scripts/run-p07-live-acceptance.ts, test_service_path), no Cloudflare queue message was ever published, delivered, or redelivered for this test — wake2 is a runner-originated HTTP replay of wake1, reusing the run created for test 02. The artifact carries no graph_run_id, no idempotency key, no timestamps, no HTTP status/transcript, no queue_message_id, no CF consumer log, no DB corroboration (run_events/receipts showing single execution), and no binding to a deployed code version. It therefore evidences at most "same-body HTTP wake replay is answered ALREADY_SETTLED by whatever code was running at an unrecorded time" — not duplicate QUEUE delivery dedupe, which is what the test name and P07_REPORT.md row 4 claim (and which architecture laws 6/7 depend on).
internal_contradictions: P07_REPORT.md claims test 4 'Duplicate queue delivery' PASS and laws 6 (at-least-once delivery) and 7 (CF ACK discipline) 'enforced', while test 02's evidence in the same bundle records dispatch-once returning {"ok":true,"delivered":0,"failed":2,"dlq":0} — zero successful queue deliveries during the very run test 4 piggybacks on.

Test 06's evidence in the same bundle records outbox last_error 'cf_queue_publish_failed status=400' and after-status OUTBOX_FAILED with queue_message_id=null — the queue publish path was demonstrably broken during the acceptance run, so no queue delivery (let alone a duplicate one) can have occurred; test 4's PASS relabels an HTTP replay as queue-delivery dedupe.

The pass predicate in run-p07-live-acceptance.ts accepts wake2.json.result === 'CLAIMED' as PASS — i.e., the test would still PASS if the duplicate were claimed and executed as new work, the exact failure mode the test exists to detect. The predicate cannot fail on the property it claims to prove, so even the recorded PASS is non-probative.

The artifact's status is PASS yet its entire evidence object is two self-reported strings ({first: CLAIMED, second: ALREADY_SETTLED}) with no run identifier, timestamp, or transcript — internally insufficient to connect the claim to any run, environment, or moment in time; queue-config.json in the bundle is a static config snapshot, not delivery evidence.

Witness receipt records deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz: at audit time /healthz exposed no commit, so the service code that emitted ALREADY_SETTLED cannot be attributed to audited kernel merge sha 060c5223 or proof-producing commit a3578d9. Live /healthz today returns commit_sha=7a64a355 and migration 003_graph_kernel_append_only_runtime_v1 (3 migrations) versus the bundle's migration.json listing only 2 — the environment has drifted since the proof, confirming the unbinding: a third party cannot establish which code produced the artifact's responses.

P07_REPORT.md's headline 'Verdict: PASS_P05 — all 9 live acceptance tests passed' is contradicted by its own embedded evidence of queue failures (delivered=0/failed=2, cf_queue_publish_failed 400) and by the independent witness tally (3 PASS, 4 FAIL, 1 INDETERMINATE, final FAIL_P05).
diagnosis.witness_rationale: INDETERMINATE is the precisely correct grade, reconstructed as follows. Not PASS: (1) claim/evidence mismatch — the test name and report claim duplicate QUEUE delivery is handled, but the producing script performs a direct HTTP wake replay with no queue involvement, and sibling evidence in the same bundle proves the queue publish path was returning 400 with delivered=0 throughout the run, so the named behavior was never exercised and could not have been; (2) the pass predicate includes 'CLAIMED' for the second wake, so the test as coded cannot distinguish dedupe from double-execution — a PASS from it carries no information about the property; (3) every datum is self-reported by the producer with zero external binding — no SHA (healthz exposed no commit at audit time, per the witness's deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE finding), no DB row, no HTTP transcript, no queue message id — so a third party cannot re-derive the conclusion without trusting the producer, which a witness must not do; (4) the deployed-code unbinding breaks attribution: even if ALREADY_SETTLED is genuine, it cannot be attributed to the audited merge sha 060c5223, and the live service has since drifted (commit 7a64a355, migration 003). Not FAIL: the recorded second-wake ALREADY_SETTLED is consistent with idempotent wake handling at the HTTP layer, nothing affirmatively shows double-execution occurred, and the dedupe-on-wake behavior may well be correct — the evidence is insufficient rather than refuting. Insufficient-to-decide plus unverifiable attribution is the definition of INDETERMINATE. If anything the grade is generous: given the test proves a different, weaker property than it names while the named property's substrate (queue delivery) was provably broken, MISLABELED/FAIL-as-scoped would also have been defensible.
diagnosis.remediation: Builder lane must (1) repair the queue publish path first — dispatch-once must show delivered>=1 with a non-null queue_message_id on the outbox row (the cf_queue_publish_failed status=400 seen in test 06 evidence makes any queue-delivery claim void); (2) rewrite test 04 to exercise the real path: trigger a run, let dispatch-once publish the wake to CF queue graph-kernel-staging, capture the outbox row (outbox_id, queue_message_id, status=OUTBOX_DELIVERED), then force a genuine redelivery of the same message (CF at-least-once redelivery via consumer retry/nack, or a second publish of the identical idempotencyKey payload) and capture the CF consumer's handling of both deliveries (wrangler tail excerpt or consumer-written delivery-receipt rows with message id and attempt numbers 1 and 2); (3) fix the pass predicate: second delivery result must be in {DUPLICATE_WAKE, ALREADY_SETTLED, ALREADY_ACTIVE}; result CLAIMED on a duplicate is FAIL, not PASS; (4) bind to deployed code: healthz now exposes commit_sha — snapshot /healthz (commit_sha, build_id, worker_id, migration versions+checksums) at test start and end inside the artifact, and require commit_sha to equal the repo SHA under audit; (5) corroborate in the DB: include the run_events rows (event_id, event_type, timestamps) for the graph_run_id showing exactly one claim/settle sequence and the duplicate-rejection event, plus receipts count=1, with the verbatim read-only SQL a third party can run; (6) record full HTTP/queue transcripts (method, path, timestamps, nonces, status codes, full response JSON) and artifact metadata (produced_at UTC, producer script commit, graph_run_id, idempotency_key, staging URL); (7) resubmit as 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' — the witness, not the builder, flips the grade.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Code binding: artifact embeds /healthz snapshots taken at test start and end, each containing commit_sha equal to the git SHA under audit, plus build_id/railway_deployment_id, worker_id, and migration versions+checksums matching origin/main migration files byte-for-byte; start and end snapshots must agree (no mid-test redeploy).

Queue actually used: outbox row snapshot for the test's wake shows status=OUTBOX_DELIVERED with non-null queue_message_id, and the dispatch-once response in the artifact shows delivered>=1, failed=0 for this message — directly refuting the delivered=0/cf_queue_publish_failed condition present in the current bundle.

Duplicate delivery witnessed at the consumer: evidence of two deliveries of the same queue_message_id/idempotencyKey to the CF consumer (attempt 1 and attempt 2), via wrangler-tail excerpt or consumer-persisted delivery records with UTC timestamps, and the consumer ACK decision for each per law 7.

Dedupe outcome externally corroborated: both kernel wake responses recorded in full (status code, complete JSON, timestamp, nonce); second result in {DUPLICATE_WAKE, ALREADY_SETTLED, ALREADY_ACTIVE} and not CLAIMED; the script's committed pass predicate excludes CLAIMED for the duplicate.

Single-execution proof in DB: artifact includes graph_run_id and idempotency_key, the run_events rows showing exactly one claim/settle sequence plus one duplicate-rejection/dedupe event, and receipts count=1 for the run — together with the verbatim read-only SQL queries so a third party with read-only DB access can re-derive the counts without rerunning anything.

Provenance and integrity: artifact carries produced_at UTC, producer identity, the commit SHA of run-p07-live-acceptance.ts that produced it, and is covered by the bundle's proof-manifest sha256 so the witness can detect post-hoc edits.

Internal consistency: no sibling evidence in the same bundle (tests 02/06 dispatch results, outbox errors) may contradict queue functionality within the test's time window; any residual failure counters must be explained and timestamped outside the window.

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: The artifact (origin/main:packages/runway-graph-kernel/proofs/p07/05_worker_termination.json) is a 3-field self-report: status=PASS, note="node_status=PENDING lease_expired_events=1", evidence={node_run_id: nr_kill_e4365096, fencing_before: 3}. Per the producing script (scripts/run-p07-live-acceptance.ts, test 5 block), what actually happened is: the test harness itself INSERTed a node_attempts row (status=LEASED, fencing_token=3, lease_owner='worker-old') and a leases row with lease_expires_at already in the past (now() - 1 second), slept 7 seconds, then re-read the row and counted NODE_LEASE_EXPIRED events. No worker process was ever started, terminated, or killed; the "termination" is a synthetic pre-expired lease injected by direct SQL from the same client that graded itself PASS. At most, the artifact is consistent with SOME process flipping a synthetic expired-lease row to PENDING within a 7-second window — it does not identify that process as the deployed reaper, does not bind the observation to any service commit, timestamp, event_id, or row image, and does not test the two laws the P07_REPORT.md claims it enforces (law 9 "Lease reaper -> PENDING + OUTBOX_PENDING": the OUTBOX_PENDING half is entirely unwitnessed; law 10 "Stale fencing rejected": fencing_before=3 is recorded but no stale-fenced request is ever attempted).
internal_contradictions: Name-vs-procedure contradiction: the test is titled and reported as 'Worker termination / reaper' (P07_REPORT.md row 5) but the script never terminates a worker — it fabricates an already-expired lease via direct DB INSERT from the test client, so the scenario the PASS is attributed to never occurred.

Disjunctive pass predicate: ok = (status=='PENDING' OR status=='FAILED' OR lease_expired_events>0). The test can self-grade PASS if the node lands in FAILED with zero expiry events, or if ANY NODE_LEASE_EXPIRED event exists on the run — three mutually inconsistent success stories collapsed into one PASS.

Event attribution gap bordering on contradiction: the NODE_LEASE_EXPIRED count is queried by graph_run_id only (the shared run from test 02, run_4ef3f648ad1f442a9dc1b9e8e2ba06e9 — which is not even recorded in this artifact's evidence), not by node_run_id nr_kill_e4365096, so 'lease_expired_events=1' is not provably about the injected lease at all.

Report law 9 vs bundle evidence: P07_REPORT.md claims 'Lease reaper -> PENDING + OUTBOX_PENDING' enforced, but the same session's bundle shows the outbox->queue leg demonstrably broken: live-tests.json test 02 note has dispatch {ok:true, delivered:0, failed:2} and test 06 evidence records last_error='cf_queue_publish_failed status=400'. No outbox row for the reaped node is evidenced, and the delivery path was failing during the run — yet 05 and the report claim plain PASS on the reaper law.

Version binding contradiction: the witness receipt records deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz at observation time (2026-07-22T13:14:04Z); current healthz now reports commit_sha=7a64a355 and migration 003, while the artifact's own sibling evidence (test 01) lists only migrations 001/002 and the witness pins proof_producing_commit=a3578d9 vs audited merge 060c5223 vs main fa2e2e07. Four different SHAs are in play and the artifact binds to none of them — the PASS cannot be attributed to the audited code.

Verdict-chain contradiction: P07_VERDICT.json/P07_REPORT.md declare PASS_P05 'all 9 live tests passed' on the strength of files like this one, but the runner writes both the per-test proofs and the verdict from the same process with no independent check — author==subject, violating the receipts law the repo itself operates under (PASS comes only from independent verifiers).
diagnosis.witness_rationale: A strict read-only witness cannot re-derive the claimed conclusion from the artifact, and the claimed conclusion is not what the test measured. Reconstruction of the FAIL: (1) The acceptance test's stated purpose — a terminated worker's lease is reaped, the node returns to PENDING with a re-enqueued OUTBOX_PENDING wake, and stale fencing is rejected — is not exercised: no worker terminated, no outbox re-enqueue evidenced, no stale-fenced call attempted. (2) The only success signals are self-reported strings from the producer; the evidence block omits the graph_run_id, the event_id, timestamps, before/after row snapshots, and reaper identity, so nothing in the artifact is externally checkable. (3) The pass predicate is a disjunction whose weakest disjunct (any NODE_LEASE_EXPIRED on a shared run) can fire without the reaper touching this node. (4) Attribution is broken at the code level: at observation time /healthz exposed no commit (witness: deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz), so even a genuine reaper transition could not be attributed to the audited SHA 060c5223; migration checksums bind only the schema. (5) Affirmative contrary evidence exists in the same bundle for the OUTBOX half of the claimed law (cf_queue_publish_failed status=400, delivered=0/failed=2). Under a standard where a test FAILs when its acceptance criteria are not established by its own evidence and part of the claimed behavior is contradicted by sibling evidence, FAIL is the correct grade — INDETERMINATE would be defensible only if the test's claim were narrowed to 'an expired synthetic lease was swept', which is not what the report claims it proves.
diagnosis.remediation: Builder lane must re-run test 05 against a version-bound deployment and produce an externally re-derivable artifact — not edit the grade. Concretely: (a) deploy staging at the audited SHA and confirm /healthz commit_sha equals it (healthz now exposes commit_sha=7a64a355, so this binding is available — the deploy must be repinned to the audited merge or the audit repinned to the deployed SHA); (b) exercise a REAL termination: have the deployed service (not the test client) grant a lease via the normal wake/claim path, kill or hard-stop that worker mid-lease, and let the deployed reaper act; (c) after reap, attempt an execute/heartbeat with the stale fencing token and capture the rejection (law 10); (d) capture the compensating OUTBOX_PENDING row and its subsequent successful queue delivery (law 9 second half — requires first fixing the cf_queue_publish_failed status=400 seen in the 06 evidence, or the reaper leg remains unprovable end-to-end); (e) regrade via an independent verifier (author != subject) and record 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' rather than self-declared PASS. Until then the witness FAIL stands and P07_VERDICT's PASS_P05 is unsupported for this test.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Version binding: artifact embeds the /healthz JSON captured at test time including commit_sha, build_id/railway_deployment_id, and migration list, with commit_sha equal to the audited kernel SHA recorded in the artifact header.

Real lease provenance: the lease under test was created by the deployed service via a signed HTTP wake/claim (full request/response transcript with timestamps embedded, secrets redacted), not by direct SQL INSERT from the test harness; the artifact records the worker identity and the termination action (signal/stop, with timestamp).

Reaper attribution: artifact embeds the NODE_LEASE_EXPIRED run_events row(s) in full — event_id, graph_run_id, node_run_id matching the leased node, event payload, created_at — plus before/after node_attempts row snapshots (status LEASED->PENDING, fencing_token increment), all queryable by a third party from the append-only ledger using identifiers present in the artifact.
Stale fencing rejection: transcript of a post-reap execute/heartbeat carrying the old fencing token (fencing_before) and the deployed service's rejection response, demonstrating law 10.

Outbox re-enqueue: the OUTBOX_PENDING row created for the reaped node (outbox_id, idempotency_key, payload_hash) and its terminal OUTBOX_DELIVERED status with queue_message_id, proving the law-9 re-wake actually delivers (not delivered=0/failed=N).

Conjunctive machine-checkable predicate: PASS requires ALL of the above (status transition AND matching expiry event for this node_run_id AND stale-fence rejection AND delivered outbox), never an OR of alternatives.

Independence and integrity: artifact carries UTC timestamps for every observation, is covered by a proof-manifest hash, and the PASS verdict is issued by an independent verifier receipt referencing this artifact — the producing runner may only write 'SUBMITTED for independent verification'.

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: The artifact evidences that on live staging the CF queue publish path was completely broken during the test run: every dispatch attempt failed with cf_queue_publish_failed status=400 (resume_dispatch delivered=0/failed=1, baseline_dispatch delivered=0/failed=2, dlq=0), the outbox row terminated at OUTBOX_FAILED with queue_message_id=null after consuming a retry (attempt 1→2), and no message was ever delivered or DLQ'd. The 'crash' (OUTBOX_CLAIMED with expired claim) and the 'reclaim' (→OUTBOX_FAILED) transitions were both injected by the test harness via direct SQL UPDATEs in run-p07-live-acceptance.ts, not executed by the deployed kernel — the script's comment 'same predicate as claimOutbox crash-resume path' is an untested assertion. The only deployed-service behavior actually observed is dispatch-once returning ok:true while failing every publish. All evidence is producer-serialized JSON written by a process holding full write access to the same DB rows it cites; there are no HTTP transcripts, timestamps, queue message IDs, service-emitted ledger events, or SHA binding, and at witness time /healthz exposed no commit (deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz). Current healthz now reports commit_sha=7a64a355 with migration 003 — matching neither the audited merge 060c5223 nor proof commit a3578d9, and drifted from the artifact's migration set (001–002), so the artifact cannot be retro-bound to any code version.
internal_contradictions: status=PASS with note 'crash-resume proven: CLAIMED(expired)→FAILED→OUTBOX_FAILED' while the embedded terminal state is the failure state itself: after.status=OUTBOX_FAILED, queue_message_id=null, last_error='cf_queue_publish_failed status=400' — the resumed dispatch failed, nothing was delivered
resume_dispatch shows delivered=0/failed=1 and baseline_dispatch delivered=0/failed=2 with dlq=0 — a test named 'outbox failure/dispatch' graded PASS while 100% of queue publishes in the run failed

the PASS predicate in run-p07-live-acceptance.ts accepts afterStatus ∈ {OUTBOX_DELIVERED, OUTBOX_FAILED, OUTBOX_DLQ} — the set of all terminal states including failure — so the test is unfalsifiable on the delivery dimension; even the script's own weaker rung (WEAK_PASS) was bypassed by this predicate

attempt accounting: reclaimed.attempt=1 then after.attempt=2 still FAILED — the retry was consumed and failed again; the max_attempts=8/DLQ exhaustion path claimed by the strengthened test was never exercised (dlq=0)

P07_REPORT.md claims architecture laws 'At-least-once delivery' and 'Queue wakes only' enforced, yet no artifact in the bundle shows a single successful queue delivery; test 02's own note records dispatch failed=2/delivered=0 and passed only via a direct signed wake that bypasses the queue

the crashed→reclaimed transitions attributed to crash-resume were produced by the harness's own SQL UPDATEs against the outbox table, so the state machine 'proven' is the test's, not the deployed service's; the comment 'same predicate as claimOutbox' is asserted, not evidenced

P07_VERDICT/report claim verdict PASS_P05 'all 9 live acceptance tests passed' derived from these self-graded statuses, while the witness receipt independently grades 4 FAIL / 1 INDETERMINATE / weak-scoped passes (final_verdict FAIL_P05)
diagnosis.witness_rationale: FAIL (not merely PASS_WEAK) is the defensible grade because the artifact does not just weakly support its claim — it affirmatively contradicts it. The behavior 06 exists to prove is that an outbox entry orphaned by a crashed dispatcher is reclaimed by the deployed kernel and eventually dispatched (delivered, or DLQ'd after exhaustion). The evidence shows: (a) delivery never occurred — every publish returned 400, delivered=0 across the whole run, dlq=0, terminal OUTBOX_FAILED with null queue_message_id; (b) the reclaim was performed by the test harness via SQL, so even the state transitions are not attributable to service code; (c) the grading predicate counts the failure state as PASS, so the PASS label carries zero information; (d) the deployed service version was unbound at observation time (no commit in healthz), and the environment has since drifted (different commit_sha, extra migration), so no third party can attribute any observed behavior to the audited SHA. PASS_WEAK would require the target behavior to be shown but poorly bound; here the target behavior is shown failing, self-injected, and unbound simultaneously. A read-only third party re-deriving from the artifact alone must conclude the dispatch pipeline was broken and the crash-resume claim untested.
diagnosis.remediation: Builder lane must (1) fix the CF queue publish 400 (the artifact's own last_error shows the wake publish rejected — likely payload/schema or binding mismatch between Railway publisher and CF queue; capture the 400 response body this time); (2) rewrite the 06 predicate so PASS requires delivery: afterStatus=OUTBOX_DELIVERED with non-null queue_message_id and delivered>=1 for the happy retry case, plus a separately labeled exhaustion case that drives attempts to max_attempts and requires status=OUTBOX_DLQ with a DLQ-side observation — OUTBOX_FAILED must map to FAIL; (3) exercise crash-resume through the deployed kernel's own code path: let the real dispatcher claim the row (observed via service-emitted run_events / claimed_by = the service worker_id from healthz, not 'crashed_worker_p07' injected by SQL), force expiry by timeout not by UPDATE, and show the service's reclaim event in the append-only ledger; (4) bind the artifact externally: record /healthz before and after (commit_sha, worker_id, build_id, migration checksums) and require commit_sha to equal the audited merge SHA; include raw HTTP transcripts (method, path, status, response body) for trigger/dispatch-once/CF publish, UTC timestamps, CF queue message IDs, and run_events event_ids so a read-only witness can re-query the ledger; (5) the runner must emit 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' rather than PASS_P05 — verdicts come from the independent witness, and P07_VERDICT must be recomputed from raw counters, not from self-graded statuses. Rerun is the builder lane's job; this diagnosis is read-only.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Artifact contains /healthz snapshots taken immediately before and after the test with commit_sha equal to the audited merge SHA on origin/main, plus build_id, railway_deployment_id, worker_id, and full migration version+checksum list matching origin/main byte-for-byte — binding service code, not just schema

Happy-path retry case: outbox row reaches status=OUTBOX_DELIVERED with a non-null queue_message_id, dispatch response shows delivered>=1 and failed=0 for that row, and the CF-side message id is recorded so the queue delivery can be cross-checked

Exhaustion case (separate, labeled): attempts driven to max_attempts by real publish failures ends at status=OUTBOX_DLQ with a DLQ queue observation (message count or DLQ message id); OUTBOX_FAILED as terminal state is graded FAIL by the runner itself

Crash-resume attribution: the CLAIMED state is created by the deployed dispatcher (claimed_by matches the service worker_id reported by healthz, never a harness-invented string), expiry occurs by elapsed time, and the reclaim is evidenced by a service-emitted event row in graph_kernel.run_events whose event_id is listed in the artifact so a read-only witness can SELECT it from the append-only ledger
Raw HTTP transcripts embedded for every service call (method, path, response status, response body, UTC timestamp, nonce) including any publish failure body — no producer-summarized-only counters

The artifact's status field is computed by a predicate that can actually fail (documented in the artifact: expected vs observed), and the top-level verdict file recomputes its tally from the per-test raw evidence; builder output is labeled 'SUBMITTED for independent verification', with PASS reserved for the independent witness

All DB-row evidence includes the exact SQL query text and result rows with timestamps, and every row cited is re-queryable read-only by a third party at diagnosis time (row still present, values matching)

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: The artifact (origin/main:packages/runway-graph-kernel/proofs/p07/07_append_only_ledger.json) evidences only this: at some unrecorded time, against some unrecorded database, a producer-run query reported that role graph_kernel_worker holds exactly INSERT and SELECT on table graph_kernel.run_events, and two booleans updateDenied=true / deleteDenied=true were set. Per the producing script (scripts/run-p07-live-acceptance.ts, test7_append_only, lines ~155-215), those booleans are ambiguous by construction: path A does SET LOCAL ROLE graph_kernel_worker and attempts UPDATE/DELETE, setting denied=true on ANY caught exception (the catch discards the error, so a type-mismatch error on `delete ... where event_id = -1`, a network hiccup, or any non-permission failure records identically to a 42501 permission denial); path B (if SET ROLE fails) merely infers denied from the ABSENCE of UPDATE/DELETE rows in a grants query filtered to grantee IN ('graph_kernel_worker','PUBLIC') — a static-catalog inference, blind to privileges inherited via role membership. The artifact does not record which path executed, no SQLSTATE, no error text, no timestamp, no DB host/database identity, no current_user, no producing commit SHA. It proves nothing about: any table other than run_events; TRUNCATE; grants held by any other role; trigger/rule-level append-only enforcement against the owner role; or — critically — whether the deployed Railway service actually connects as graph_kernel_worker rather than the privileged owner role the acceptance script itself used (DB_URL, which successfully INSERTed across six tables in test 1). What partially rescues the claim: the witness receipt independently bound the SCHEMA via migration checksums (001=be6a0865.., 002=d5128833.. byte-for-byte match to origin/main), and 002_graph_kernel_roles_migrations_v1 is the roles migration, so the grant configuration is re-derivable read-only from audited source — the narrow property "worker role lacks UPDATE/DELETE grants on run_events" is credible and third-party re-derivable. But deployed service CODE was unbound at audit time (witness: deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz), so nothing attributes runtime write-path behavior to the audited merge SHA 060c5223. (Context, not part of the audited artifact: live /healthz TODAY exposes commit_sha=7a64a355 and a new migration 003_graph_kernel_append_only_runtime_v1 — the binding gap has since been partially addressed, but with a commit and migration that postdate and are not covered by the audited artifact.)
internal_contradictions: Artifact status PASS with note 'worker cannot UPDATE/DELETE run_events' overstates its own evidence: the denied booleans are set true on any exception (error swallowed, no SQLSTATE captured) or on mere grant-absence in a filtered catalog query, so the artifact cannot distinguish a witnessed permission denial from an unrelated error or an inference — a self-declared PASS resting on undiscriminated failure modes.

The producing script's fallback path computes updateDenied/deleteDenied from a grants query restricted to grantee IN ('graph_kernel_worker','PUBLIC'); privileges inherited through role membership or held by any other role are invisible, so 'denied' can be true while the effective privilege exists — the evidence schema contradicts the universality of the claim in the note.

P07_REPORT.md declares 'Verdict: PASS_P05 — all 9 live acceptance tests passed' while the same bundle's live-tests.json embeds live failure counters: test 02 note 'dispatch={ok:true,delivered:0,failed:2,dlq:0}' and test 06 'last_error: cf_queue_publish_failed status=400' with delivered=0 — a PASS headline over embedded delivery failures, which the independent witness graded FAIL (02, 05, 06, 09), FAIL_P05 overall.

The claim is 'append-only ledger' but the acceptance run's own test 1 demonstrates the credentials in GRAPH_KERNEL_DATABASE_URL freely INSERT into run_events and five other tables as a privileged role; nothing in the bundle shows the deployed service connects as graph_kernel_worker, so the tested role and the runtime role are not shown to be the same principal — the property proven (one role lacks two grants) does not entail the property claimed (the ledger is append-only for the running system).

bundle/migration.json and the artifact bind to migrations 001+002 only, while the report claims a complete live commissioning; at audit time /healthz exposed no commit SHA (witness: deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz), so the bundle simultaneously claims 'live staging' proof and lacks the one field that ties live behavior to any code version.
diagnosis.witness_rationale: PASS_SCOPED is the correct strict-witness grade, reconstructed as follows. Upgrade-from-FAIL justification: unlike tests 02/05/06/09, the core property here is a database-catalog fact that an independent read-only witness can re-derive without trusting the producer — migration 002_graph_kernel_roles_migrations_v1 is checksum-bound byte-for-byte to origin/main (the witness's own deployed_schema_binding finding), and that audited SQL defines the worker grants; a read-only grants query against the same Supabase project reproduces INSERT+SELECT-only for graph_kernel_worker on run_events. So the claim is not merely self-reported: it has one genuine external binding (schema checksum), which is more than a producer-authored JSON PASS. Downgrade-from-PASS justification (the 'SCOPED' qualifiers): (1) the enforcement evidence is grants-only and single-table/single-role — no denial transcript, no SQLSTATE, unrecorded execution path, error-swallowing that manufactures denied=true from any exception; (2) the artifact carries zero binding metadata of its own (no timestamp, DB identity, commit, or transcript), so as a standalone document it is unverifiable — it passes only by leaning on the witness's separate schema-checksum work; (3) attribution is broken above the schema layer: deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz means no one can attribute the running service's write path to the audited merge 060c5223, and nothing proves the service's runtime DB principal is the restricted worker role rather than the privileged owner role the test harness itself used; (4) the surrounding bundle self-declares PASS while embedding live failures (delivered=0/failed=2, cf_queue_publish_failed 400), which destroys producer credibility and forces every producer-authored boolean to be discounted to what is independently re-derivable. What survives that discount is exactly: 'the audited schema restricts one named role to INSERT+SELECT on run_events' — a true, scoped, schema-level property. PASS for the schema property, SCOPED because runtime enforcement, principal identity, code attribution, and ledger-wide coverage are all unwitnessed. Not FAIL/INDETERMINATE because the scoped property is independently derivable; not plain PASS because the builder's broader claim ('append-only ledger', live) is not.
diagnosis.remediation: The builder lane must regenerate the P07 test-7 artifact so a read-only third party can verify without rerunning, and must stop self-issuing PASS (repo law: builders write 'SUBMITTED for independent verification'; PASS comes from the independent witness). Concretely: (1) Fix the probe in scripts/run-p07-live-acceptance.ts test7_append_only — record which path executed (set_role_succeeded boolean), capture the verbatim error message and SQLSTATE for each denied UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE attempt, and treat only SQLSTATE 42501 (insufficient_privilege) as a denial; any other error class must yield INDETERMINATE, never denied=true. Remove the grantee IN (...) filter and enumerate ALL grantees plus effective privileges via has_table_privilege() across role memberships. (2) Embed binding metadata in the artifact itself: UTC timestamp, current_database(), inet_server_addr()/project ref, current_user before and after SET ROLE, the migration version+checksum list read in the same session, the producing git commit SHA, and the bundle manifest hash. (3) Close the principal gap: expose the service's runtime DB principal (e.g. /healthz reporting current_user/session role, or a redacted env manifest showing the DATABASE_URL role) proving the deployed service connects as graph_kernel_worker, not the owner. (4) Close the code-attribution gap: /healthz must report commit_sha (live healthz now does — commit_sha=7a64a355, plus a new migration 003_graph_kernel_append_only_runtime_v1 suggesting trigger-level enforcement was added); the refreshed artifact must be produced against a deployment whose healthz commit_sha equals the audited merge SHA, and migration 003's checksum must be witness-verified against origin/main the same way 001/002 were. (5) Widen scope to match the claim: test every ledger table named in canon (or retitle the claim to run_events only), include TRUNCATE, and if append-only is meant to hold against the owner role, evidence the trigger/rule that enforces it (attempt a privileged UPDATE and capture its trigger-raised rejection). Then submit for the independent witness to re-grade.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Artifact records probe path explicitly: set_role_succeeded=true and, for each of UPDATE, DELETE, TRUNCATE on every ledger table, a verbatim denial transcript with SQLSTATE 42501 and the exact SQL attempted; any non-42501 error must be recorded as INDETERMINATE, not as a denial.

Artifact is self-binding: contains UTC timestamp, current_database(), server/project identity, current_user before and after SET ROLE, producing git commit SHA, and the same-session migration version+checksum list, so a third party can match checksums to origin/main migration files byte-for-byte without trusting the producer.

Grant enumeration is unfiltered and effective: all grantees on each ledger table listed (no grantee IN filter), plus has_table_privilege('graph_kernel_worker', tbl, 'UPDATE'/'DELETE'/'TRUNCATE') results covering inherited role membership, all false.

Runtime principal bound: read-only evidence (healthz field or redacted env manifest) that the deployed Railway service's DB session role is graph_kernel_worker — i.e., the restricted role tested is the principal that actually writes the ledger in production paths.

Deployed code bound: /healthz reports commit_sha and it equals the audited merge SHA for the commissioning under grade; the witness can curl it read-only and match it to origin/main (eliminating deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz).

If append-only is claimed against ALL roles (including owner), the artifact evidences the enforcing trigger/rule from migration 003_graph_kernel_append_only_runtime_v1 (checksum-verified against origin/main) plus a captured trigger-raised rejection of a privileged UPDATE attempt; otherwise the artifact's note explicitly scopes the claim to the worker role.

The builder-side status field reads 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' with a receipt_id; PASS appears only in the independent witness's receipt (author != subject), and the witness can re-derive every boolean in the artifact from the transcripts and catalog data embedded in it without rerunning anything.

diagnosis.artifact_actually_evidences: The artifact (proofs/p07/09_server_side_hold.json, produced by scripts/run-p07-live-acceptance.ts) evidences only this: the builder's own runner made two HMAC-signed direct POSTs to /internal/v1/graph-kernel/wake with requestedCapability=deploy.promote — one with no approval header, one with header x-nf-approval-id: "does-not-exist" — and transcribed two JSON response bodies: {result:"HOLD_REQUIRED", reason:"missing_approval"} and {result:"HOLD_REQUIRED", reason:"malformed_approval"}, plus graph_run_id run_ece0795e236f4baa8fd9c3cbd6a7fc4b. Nothing more. No HTTP status codes, no timestamps, no raw request/response transcript, no server-side DB rows (no run_events HOLD event, no node_attempts state snapshot, no approvals-ledger row), and no positive-path probe showing a valid approval releases the hold. The precondition node row (n_deploy PENDING) was inserted by the harness itself via direct DB write (storePutNodePending), not produced by the kernel. The responding service's code version was unrecorded: the witness receipt shows deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz, and migration checksums bind schema only. A live healthz read today returns commit_sha 7a64a355 and migration 003 — matching neither the audited merge 060c5223/proof commit a3578d9 nor the bundle's migration set (001,002) — confirming the environment mutates and nothing pins the observed HOLD responses to the audited code. The artifact therefore evidences that SOME unidentified code version returned HOLD_REQUIRED twice to the producer; it does not evidence a server-side, ledger-governed HOLD enforced by the audited kernel.
internal_contradictions: Status PASS and P07_VERDICT 'PASS_P05 — all 9 live acceptance tests passed' while sibling evidence from the SAME run session embeds live failures: 02_complete_motor_path PASS with dispatch {ok:true, delivered:0, failed:2} and 06_outbox_failure PASS with after.status=OUTBOX_FAILED, last_error='cf_queue_publish_failed status=400', delivered=0 — the real queue delivery path was failing during the session, yet the report claims queue/at-least-once laws (4,6,7) enforced.

'Server-side HOLD' is the claim, but the server-side precondition state (node_attempts row for n_deploy, PENDING) was manufactured client-side by the test harness via direct DB insert (storePutNodePending) using producer credentials — the kernel never scheduled the node whose hold is being 'proven'.

The note labels the second probe as the bad-approval case, but reason='malformed_approval' for id 'does-not-exist' shows only input-format rejection; a well-formed-but-nonexistent/revoked approval (actual ledger lookup denial) was never exercised, so the artifact's own reason string contradicts the claimed coverage.

The test is billed as 'live' acceptance, yet the wake bypassed the deployed delivery path entirely (direct signed POST instead of CF queue consumer) — while the same session's evidence shows the CF queue publish path returning 400 on every attempt.

P07_REPORT claims the healthz surface is commissioning-grade, but at witness observation time healthz exposed no commit SHA (deployed_sha=UNVERIFIABLE_no_commit_in_healthz), so the verdict's implicit attribution of behavior to the audited kernel merge (060c5223) is unsupported by its own bundle; the bundle's migration list (001,002) also no longer matches the live service (003), demonstrating unpinned environment drift.

Repo receipt law says builders write 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' and PASS comes only from independent verifiers or deterministic gates (author ≠ subject); this artifact is the producer grading its own probes PASS — the verdict field itself violates the canon it is offered under.
diagnosis.witness_rationale: The witness FAIL is the correct strict-grade for three stacked reasons. (1) Attribution failure: the acceptance criterion is 'the audited kernel enforces server-side HOLD on staging', and with healthz exposing no commit SHA at observation time, no evidence binds the two HOLD_REQUIRED responses to the audited merge 060c5223 — schema checksums bind the database, not the service code, and the environment demonstrably drifts (today's healthz shows a different commit and an extra migration). (2) Zero discriminating power: both probes are negative-only; an endpoint hardcoded to return HOLD_REQUIRED for deploy.promote — i.e., a broken or stubbed gate — passes this test identically. Without a valid-approval release probe, the test cannot distinguish 'governed hold' from 'dead endpoint', so even a perfectly captured transcript could not establish the claim; the test design, not just the capture, fails acceptance. (3) Everything is self-reported: response bodies transcribed by the producer, precondition state hand-inserted by the producer into the DB, verdict emitted by the producer — no HTTP transcript, no server-appended ledger rows, no third-party-queryable state, so a read-only witness can re-derive nothing. Under the receipt doctrine (self-declared PASS proves nothing; PASS only from independent verification), and given that HOLD is a founder-gate-critical safety control where an unproven gate must be treated as absent, FAIL rather than INDETERMINATE is defensible: the test as designed cannot prove its claim in principle, and the same session's own embedded evidence (queue publish 400s) actively contradicts the 'live commissioned' framing the PASS depends on.
diagnosis.remediation: Builder lane must re-produce the 09 artifact as a witness-verifiable evidence package, not a self-graded verdict. Concretely: (1) Bind code version — capture /healthz (which now exposes commit_sha, build_id, railway_deployment_id) immediately before and after the probes and require commit_sha == the audited merge SHA under grade; embed both snapshots in the artifact. (2) Record full redacted HTTP transcripts for every probe: method, path, request headers including x-nf-approval-id (HMAC values redacted), RFC3339 timestamps, HTTP status codes, and complete response bodies. (3) Run a four-way discrimination matrix: (a) missing approval → HOLD_REQUIRED/missing_approval; (b) malformed approval id → HOLD_REQUIRED/malformed_approval; (c) well-formed but nonexistent-or-revoked approval id → HOLD_REQUIRED with a ledger-lookup denial reason; (d) valid approval recorded through the governed approvals path → hold released and the node proceeds (CLAIMED / state transition). Case (d) is what proves the gate is conditional rather than hardcoded. (4) Capture server-side state: run_events rows for the graph_run_id showing a service-appended HOLD event (with worker_id) after each denial, node_attempts remaining PENDING/HELD until the valid approval and transitioning after it, and the approvals-ledger row for case (d) — include row snapshots plus the exact read-only SQL so a witness with read credentials re-derives them. (5) Precondition provenance: create the held node via the kernel's own trigger/plan path; if harness seeding is unavoidable, declare it as seeded state in the artifact. (6) Deliver at least one denial probe through the real CF queue consumer (queue_message_id + consumer ack evidence), since direct signed POSTs bypass the deployed wake path; any queue publish failure in the session must fail the run, never sit inside PASS evidence. (7) Verdict discipline: artifact states 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' with a receipt_id and a sha256 manifest over all evidence files; PASS is issued only by the independent witness after re-derivation.
witnessable_acceptance_criteria: Artifact contains healthz snapshots (before/after probes) whose commit_sha equals the audited kernel merge SHA recorded in the artifact, with build_id/railway_deployment_id — a third party can curl /healthz and compare.

Every probe is recorded as a full redacted HTTP transcript (method, path, headers incl. x-nf-approval-id, timestamps, HTTP status code, complete response body) — a third party can re-derive the denial/release logic without trusting summary strings.

Discrimination matrix present: missing approval, malformed id, well-formed-but-nonexistent/revoked id, and VALID approval all probed; the valid-approval case shows the hold releasing (node transitions/CLAIMED), proving the gate is conditional rather than a hardcoded refusal.

Server-appended run_events rows (with worker_id) for the test graph_run_id show a HOLD event per denial and a release event after valid approval; node_attempts snapshots show PENDING/HELD persisting through denials and advancing only after approval; the approvals-ledger row for the valid case is included — each with the exact read-only SQL used, re-runnable by a witness with read credentials.
Precondition state provenance is declared: the held node was created via the kernel trigger/plan path, or any harness-seeded rows are explicitly labeled as seeded in the artifact.

At least one denial was delivered via the real CF queue consumer with queue_message_id and consumer ack evidence; the session contains zero unexplained queue publish failures embedded inside passing evidence.

The artifact declares 'SUBMITTED for independent verification' (no self-issued PASS), carries a receipt_id and a sha256 manifest over all evidence files, and the committed manifest hash matches what the witness recomputes from the files.
